title: Fanout Exchanges and True Publish/Subscribe · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers fanout exchanges and true publish/subscribe into a new fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.
Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility.
Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 1879 in a accessible municipal task intake with two worker pools. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility, keep Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Late Subscriber Semantics, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A new fanout binding automatically receives messages published before it existed.

stimulus.title: Late Subscriber Semantics failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A new fanout binding automatically receives messages published before it existed.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Late Subscriber Semantics condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Late Subscriber Semantics
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Late Subscriber Semantics: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab messaging team handles case 1463 in a accessible municipal task intake with two worker pools. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Late Subscriber Semantics.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Lab Late Subscriber Semantics, keep Late Subscriber Semantics connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path.
